hypothesis.summary: Run the ylong_base benchmark entry on ylong_runtime current-thread mode to test whether readiness/parking overhead in the global runtime explains nested HTTPS proxy latency.
hypothesis.scope: diagnostic benchmark substrate only; not a production ylong_http_client protocol or API change

temporary_candidate_changes: Enabled ylong_runtime current_thread_runtime dependency feature for the benchmark build.
Added a Python harness ylong_runtime result dimension.
Removed these candidate changes after measurement because they did not satisfy S9/SOTA thresholds and would otherwise leave a non-winning diagnostic knob in the deliverable.

verification_commands.output_summary: 7 bench-bin unit tests passed after candidate rollback; existing warnings only

verification_commands.command: python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --bench-bin /tmp/ylong-http-sota-target/release/https_proxy_bench --scenario https-over-https-proxy --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --ylong-runtime current-thread --baseline libcurl --phase-timing --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/ylong-current-thread-candidate --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/ylong-current-thread-candidate
verification_commands.output_summary: temporary diagnostic run completed before rollback

verification_commands.command: python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --bench-bin /tmp/ylong-http-sota-target/release/https_proxy_bench --scenario https-over-https-proxy --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --ylong-runtime global --baseline libcurl --phase-timing --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/ylong-global-runtime-control --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/ylong-global-runtime-control
verification_commands.output_summary: temporary same-parameter global-runtime control completed before rollback

counterexample_gate_observation.summary: The 200-request row has local throughput improvement, but the 1000-request row regresses throughput, median latency, tail latency, CPU, and RSS versus libcurl. A single short-run win is therefore insufficient SOTA evidence.
counterexample_gate_observation.sota_effect: fails: geomean throughput remains below libcurl and resource/tail metrics regress
counterexample_gate_observation.s9_effect: does not provide causal production performance evidence for a winning ylong_http_client change

decision.next_action: S9 remains open; any further candidate must show causal, production-grade improvement with geomean throughput leadership and no p95/CPU/RSS regression across the approved HTTPS proxy matrix.
